occupation_definition: Perform any combination of tasks in a United States Postal Service (USPS) post office, such as receive letters and parcels; sell postage and revenue stamps, postal cards, and stamped envelopes; fill out and sell money orders; place mail in pigeon holes of mail rack or in bags; and examine mail for correct postage. Includes postal service clerks employed by USPS contractors.

breadcrumb.occupation_title: Material Recording, Scheduling, Dispatching, and Distributing Workers

siblings.occupation_title: Postal Service Mail Sorters, Processors, and Processing Machine Operators
